events.title: Professional Sake Training: Sake Server Certification
events.description:
Event Details
Sign up for this class At the Sake Studies Center:
https://www.sakestudiescenter.com/courses#id=sake-server-certification
This is a one day in person seminar from 10am to 5pm with a 1 hour lunch break.
Level up your service skills in sake! This one day sake training certification course will teach you all the ins and outs of proper service and sales for sake in your your restaurant or retail shop. There is no prerequisite and this course is open to anyone wishing to get training in professional service of sake. The course consists of four modules.
1) Introduction to sake & Brewery Tour
2) sake sales techniques
3) sake service and presentation
4) sake menus and pairing
After the one day training course in completed, you’ll have to option to complete an online course review and online exam within two weeks time. Upon successful completion of the review and exam, you’ll receive the Sake Studies Center “Sake Server Certification” diploma.
As a graduate of our professional Sake Server Certification, you’ll also have ongoing access to our free online sake community for the sake industry. Ask questions, and stay in touch with your fellow sake students and industry leaders.
Meet your Teacher
Timothy Sullivan is the Director of Education and lead instructor for classes at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura. He has over 16 years of experience teaching about sake and in 2007, he was awarded the prestigious title of Sake Samurai by the Japan Sake Brewers Association. Timothy brings a wealth of knowledge as well as practical brewing experience to the classroom. Whether for sake beginners or for industry professionals, Timothy strives to make all his classes and tasting fun and engaging.
events.vendor: Professional Sake Training: Sake Server Certification

events.title: Zine Club
events.description:
Join us every two weeks for Zine Club, a gathering dedicated to DIY bookmaking, self-publishing, creative experimentation, and community. Whether you’re new to zines or working on an ongoing project, this is a space to explore ideas, share work, and be creative in community with other artists, writers, activists, and creatives of all kinds.
Each begins with a quick demo of a technique or process you might incorporate in your own work. Explore a wide range of materials and techniques available in our studio, including collage, drawing, image transfer, stenciling, and printmaking.
Zine Club is led by Lista Studios instructor Wil Bigio, a graphic designer, illustrator, and dedicated doodler based in New York.
Details:
Meets every other Wednesday (first and third of the month) from 6:00 pm – 8:30 pm
Basic materials provided (paper, collage supplies, and printmaking tools)
events.vendor: Zine Club

events.title: New York Knicks NBA Finals Game 1 Watch Party
events.description:
RSVP Here
Watch the Knickerbockers try to capture the NBA title! Enjoy the game under the stars with full concert sound on our massive 16 foot screen!
Come watch the New York Knicks in Game 1 of the NBA Finals on our 16-foot wide projection screen and listen to the game in full concert sound under the stars in Sunset Park, Brooklyn.
This event is free with RSVP, ages 8 and up are welcome. There will be drink specials, all night, but don’t forget to take advantage of the 2-for-1 drink ticket when you check out!
Limited seating (about 100 seats), tons of standing room.
While we do our best to gauge the number of RSVPs, entry is still on a first-come, first-served basis, so please arrive early! Once we hit capacity, we will only let people in on an in and out basis!
No outside beverages. There will be drink specials all night long from our courtyard bar. Feel free to bring in food from the adjoining food hall!
Event Info
Date: June 3, 2026
Doors: 7pm
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
events.vendor: New York Knicks NBA Finals Game 1 Watch Party

events.title: Upcycled Plastic Earring Workshop
events.description:
Turn trash into treasures in this sustainable jewelry-making workshop in NYC! In this one-of-a-kind jewelry-making experience, where sustainability meets creativity, you will turn plastic waste into wearable art!
Experience wellness for our mind and the Earth through this hands-on workshop. In this creative, eco-friendly workshop, you’ll design custom earrings using colorful shapes crafted from recycled NYC plastics. This is your chance to express your imagination while learning about material circularity and embracing sustainability.
This workshop is guided by Rina Shin, and in collaboration with Precious Plastic NYC (PPNYC). Rina is a Color and Material Designer with over 15 years of professional experience working with brands like Microsoft and Logitech. PPNYC is a New York-based initiative dedicated to transforming plastic waste into sustainable materials through community-focused, creative workshops.
Your workshop will include all the materials needed to create a pair of earrings (including hardware for clip-ons). Feel free to BYO additional hardware to turn your creations into necklaces, charms, or anything else you dream up.
The workshop will be hosted at Brooklyn Creative Reuse 254 36th Street, Space C244 (2nd Floor) Friday June 5th, 7-9pm.
events.vendor: Upcycled Plastic Earring Workshop

events.title: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour
events.description:
Set against the iconic backdrop of Sunset Park’s historic waterfront, the massive, revitalized structures of Industry City form a breathtaking stage for an exclusive culinary journey. This premium food and cocktail tour is a curated exploration of taste and craft, transforming an evening into a memorable, high-end experience.
Your adventure begins with artistic bonbons at Lilac Chocolates, explores gourmet imports at Sahadi’s, and includes a refined whiskey tasting at Fort Hamilton Distillery, followed by a sophisticated sake flight. Savor additional artisanal discoveries within the awe-inspiring industrial halls.
Perfect as a distinctive date night, this tour is also fully customizable for private groups and corporate retreats. We specialize in tailoring this engaging experience for team-building and client entertainment, making it an ideal one-day corporate outing. A sophisticated mini-vacation for locals and a profound discovery for visitors.
events.vendor: Industry City Gourmet Food and Drinks Tour

events.title: Half Step – Grateful Dead Tribute
events.description:
Tickets Here
Industry City and Get It Right Music in association with Murmrr Presents are thrilled to welcome back Half Step – Long Island’s premier Grateful Dead tribute act!
This show will feature two full sets of Half Step!
Event Info
Date: June 7, 2026
Doors: 1:45pm
Set 1: 2:00pm
Set 2: 3:45pm
Tickets: $20 per person in advance/$25 day of show plus fees
Address: Industry City Bandshell Stage, Courtyard 1/2 at Industry City in Brooklyn New York between 36th and 37th street and 2nd and 3rd Avenues.
ENTER 238 36th Street, Brooklyn, NY on your GPS device
The venue is located in an open-air courtyard featuring a full concert sound stage. Fans can get affordable drinks from the venue’s bar and order food from over 17 options inside the adjoining food hall. Concert is rain or shine.
How to get here:
Take the D, N, R lines to 36th street station in Brooklyn and walk west one block to Industry City. 3 stops from Manhattan. 30 minutes from Herald Square.
From Long Island, take the LIRR to Atlantic Terminal then take the D, N or R train to 36th street.
More About Half Step
Half Step is a group of dedicated fans & students of the Grateful Dead, capturing the spirit, improvisation, sound & complexity of Grateful Dead music. Established in 2004 on Long Island, Half Step has been mastering their craft while winning over crowds of veteran Deadheads, newcomers and non-Dead fans alike.
What makes Half Step stand out from the rest?
Like the Grateful Dead themselves, the band is constantly pushing the envelope, growing together as musicians and performers. Only true fans that have experienced and studied the music with an extreme level of dedication and passion can accurately represent the superb body of work that the Grateful Dead have to offer.
When seeing Half Step live, one can clearly see the attention to detail that the band has in putting on a viable Grateful Dead experience. They stay true to the traditional show format and play sets of tunes in a sequence that could likely have been performed by the Dead themselves years ago. The members of Half Step also use similar instruments, amplifiers and effects to further authenticate the Grateful Dead sound.
In the band’s long history, they have undergone and overcome numerous incarnations. With each new member they come closer to refining their approach while consistently raising their standards. Half Step’s current line up is by far the most seasoned and prepared to bring their show to the next level.
As musicians, Half Step has what it takes to present the Dead’s music to even the most critical of fans. Anyone who has experienced the Grateful Dead in one way or another have formed a unique impression of their music. Half Step has the rare privilege to be in the position to share their collective impression with you.
events.vendor: Half Step – Grateful Dead Tribute

events.title: Sake for Travelers to Japan
events.description:
Planning a trip to Japan? Sake is everywhere – and knowing how to navigate it will transform your travel experience! Join us at the Sake Studies Center at Brooklyn Kura in NYC for an immersive sake class designed specifically for sake-loving travelers headed to Japan. We’ll give you the knowledge, vocabulary, and confidence to order sake like a local, visit breweries off the beaten path, and discover the styles you love most.
Set inside our state-of-the-art NYC sake brewery, this is a uniquely hands-on, fun and interactive learning experience. You’ll tour the brewery, taste sake fresh from the press, and work your way through a guided flight of 5 premium sakes — including selections straight from Japan. Our Sake 101 presentation will cover everything you need to know, and you’ll leave with practical tools you’ll need to understand and order sake the moment you land in Tokyo. We’ll also walk you through the do’s and don’ts of sake etiquette — from how to pour for your table-mates to the customs and rituals that show respect and make the best impression wherever in Japan your travels may take you.
What’s included:
Insider tips on how to find and visit sake breweries in Japan
A vocabulary cheat sheet so you can confidently order the sake that suits you perfectly
Sake etiquette and cultural customs to help you drink with confidence and respect
Sake 101 presentation covering all the sake basics you need to know
Guided tasting of 5 premium sakes, including selections from Japan
Guided tour through our NYC sake brewery
Sake 101 Handouts to take with you on your travels
Light nibbles to pair with your sakes
events.vendor: Sake for Travelers to Japan

events.title: Blkrose Productions Presents: Isiah Kelly, Sydney Castillo, Just Nesh Hosted By Sinck
events.description:
Doors open at 5:30PM

Sydney Castillo is a stand-up comedian, actor, producer, and writer. He most recently wrote on HBO’s "Game Theory" and VH1’s "Celebrity Squares." He was part of the most recent season of "Wild N' Out." Sydney's written for and appeared in numerous shows, ranging from the BET Awards, Comedy Central's "Hood Adjacent" and "Marlon" on NBC, now airing on Netflix. Sydney’s stand-up can be seen on HBO MAX’S "The Headliners," and can be seen headlining around the country.
